column27: Comments

column2: Mephisto Floods the Planet with Melted Glaciers
column27: This showed why Black Cat has some of the best cards in the game. With Daredevil revealing top cards I knew which player had the better top card.  Using black cat to get the 3 attack from colossus without gaining the wound was great. Each hero in the setup pulled their own weight. First game never even got a single wound thanks to Colossus and the MK heroes.

Having had a rough time with the scheme in the past I thought about using hero’s that would interact with the villains deck to delay the twists but alas when they didn’t seem to gel I thought about trying to out pace the twists.
 Therefore went with a yellow and black deck of Moon Knight, Punisher and Mr Fantastic for the left with Green/Red on the right of Colossus and Wolverine, first game went well yet only saw Moon Knights rare near the end of the game which took longer then expected yet the twists were kind enough to stay away.
 Game two used the same settings but the twists came out quicker which made for a tense finish but ultimately another victory, whether or not it’s enough to keep in the top half time will tell.

I picked Photon to add some Blue heroes to the mix, preparing for Laser Maze and Living Laser, and also I thought Hyperspeed and forced-discard cards would at least make having Wounds less of an issue. I picked Daredevil because with Mephisto putting Wounds on top of the deck, there'd be a fair number of times I'd know what card was on top of my deck.

Game 1 was probably the most efficient Legendary game I've ever played; everything ticked right. There were plenty of times where I had a choice to "reveal something or gain a Wound" and I chose the Wound, either for Colossus's Invulnerability to turn it into card draw or for cranking up Daredevil's Radar Sense. Hit Mephisto the first time on like turn 8 and had it all wrapped up in 22 turns. Colossus and Daredevil make a fun combo, especially with Mephisto!

Game 2 didn't go nearly as well, and I'm mostly frustrated because I'm not sure why. Never got the good deck synergies, lots of turns with wasted Attack or Recruit power, etc. I did manage to gum up HQ with expensive rares so that Scheme Twists weren't that bad - I got to 6 Twists in that game and still only had like 10 or 11 KO'd Heroes, so I was in no danger of losing. But it took way longer than Game 1 and I had a Villain Escape, so clearly not the game to report.

MVP was Colossus, especially in Game 1. Daredevil worked well too but the combo was great. Photon was probably a mistake, although having some Blue Heroes saved a few wounds and Ultraviolet Radiation did help hit Mephisto once or twice.

So the next Mastermind dealing with wounds again. Both attempts took exactly 29 turns, but the first attempt had more VP. Some turns were wasted and I couldn't do anything. Too bad I couldn't use Sidekicks here!

This was the next Mastermind dealing with wounds. Had a rough start with several strikes and therefore many wounds. But once I had built up my decks, the game went pretty smooth. I aborted my second attempt when I had an early stupid escape. Too bad I couldn't use Sidekicks here!
I was really hoping to go faster, but the wounds were kinda a pain.

Fastball special on the left and Versatile tech on the right. Needed more KO cards in my tech deck though. Probably could have saved Domino and Forge for later in the tournament but got wrapped up in an X-Force team.
We enjoyed having one of the most helpful henchmen villains, Mandarin’s Rings!

With all the card draw from Wolverine, and the yellow/black coordination of Moon Knight, Mr Fantastic, was a no bracket to bring along. Domino was great support, but Mr Fantastic was definitely the MVP here!
Man, Mephisto sucks! I felt my hero strategy would be sound but my first game was a total slog. Second game had a great start, which just goes to show that it all comes down to the shuffle.

Very tricky villan deck and scheme twist. Have to manage the heros in the HQ and keep vilans from flying out of the city. Got a bit lucky not seeing any master strikes early so I could get a couple of hits on bastion at 4. The master strike abilities were not exactly punishing

column27: Difficult round
Artifacts & Shards were crucial

My first attempt I was railroaded by schemes early and all my heroes were framed for murder....my second attempt I put in M and Stepford Cuckoos and cloned my way to a pretty decisive victory early...I think I shuffled better second game too.

Fun game! Went with M and the Stepford gals mainly because all M cards cost three. worked out pretty well, the HP was easy to recruit to avoid the schemes. Drak's artifact "knives of the hunter" enabled M to hit her formation easily. Danger sense helped alot as well. Over all great fun game and not as challenging as i anticipated.

Never faced Bastion before, and actively dislike him after this. Too many moving parts to follow each turn. Failed first attempt and had escapes in second. You have city with two spots free, and Supergiant just has to find sentinels....

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: OMG! Supergiant ambush bring another Supergiant which bring Sentinel ONE which bring another Sentinel. Two escaped villains from this combo. Rocket generated many shards to get victory at least but Bastion with his multiplying is always long run game.

Pretty tough setup! Hard to hit so many masterminds before the timer scheme with lots of deck thinning effects. I had to do Rictor and Shatterstar and tried a Guardians side, getting the twist to wiff once just wasn't enough.
Decent setup, but some bad luck made it really hard to win. Probably could have spent some more time on hero choice. Look out loser's bracket, here I come!

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: None

We weren't sure whether or not to switch a hero cause our first game went alright, but we thought it over for a bit and decided Ant-Man deserved a chance. Size changing helped recruit a TON plus when so much was empowered by tech it's almost like they were hardly empowered.

Brought Lady Thor to support Captain Marvel, and Princess Shuri to help Jocasta. Her uncommon's HQ cycle was also very helpful.

Got fairly lucky and Klaw was empowered by Instinct most of the game, with Venom Rocket's common and Shuri's rare being the only instinct cards available! Which meant Jocasta's employment was always far stronger!
Lady Thor and Captain Marvel for player 1 and Ant Man and Jocasta for player 2. They really worked well with each other.
Had to take quite a bit of time to take out the evolved ultrons that filled the city so both attempts took longer than expected. After that it was just cleanup with the mastermind

Luck was more important than skill for this setup. Because of the Scheme Twists entering the City it is very possible to get overwhelmed very quickly! Empower mechanic is very fun though, both for Villain and for Heroes.

There was a sense of urgency in this one, but we always managed to keep up. The setup was very interesting as well. Loved this setup and how the mastermind and the scheme both used similar mechanics, forcing us to work around them. We decided to acknowledge that twists were just always 9s with our hero choices.

We had bad luck both games with the Villains coming fast and furious. We only had one Master Strike and one Bystander in our second game (the one we submitted) to give us reprieve the whole game and Ultron was fully powered pretty quick. But it was fun to have to take turns managing the city the whole game.

Shuri's choice empowerment is just such a crazy help. Lady Thor really didn't pill her weight but on game 2, even though Wonder Man was more helpful, Klaw and the Ultrons took all of the ramp cards from Shuri and Captain Marvel. In both games Queen hugged all the rares lol
This was really fun. I tried to pick heroes with minimal dual traits as that would increase Klaw and evolved ultron HPs. The strategy seemed to pay off :-).
Crazy good synergy, we went through a nail biter the first round and then had much quicker second

column2: Thanos Provokes the Sovereign War Fleet
column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: No early masterstrikes and the scheme allowing me to get Caps rare pretty really on sealed Thanos’s Doom

I thought I wouldn't need much KO since the henchman and villains had it. But I didn't see many of those.  BP and WandVision really under performed so I changed them out for the 2nd attempt. Player 2 made the sacrifice play and frequently coordinated, and kept losing their stones to masterstrikes. Player 1 had a good Gamora deck and KO'd all their starting cards. Cap was pulling his weight. Black Widow didn't show except for a single copy of Mission Accomplished. I'm disappointed I used both Black Widow and Black Panther to not much effect.
I thought for sure that Tony Stark was going to be trash, but a lot of his cards came up early and helped get the ball Rollin.

Rough to decide between my two games, ended up choosing the one with more escapes. Last tournament that was my downfall, but I guess I never learn. Super fun setup. Gamora, Captain Marvel, and Black Panther are an insane combo. It may have been a mistake to not choose Black Widow, but hopefully that risk pays off.

That first game....it was basically over by turn 6 due to a really bad shuffle. Turn two went way better than expected. I was able to get the hand of BP/Cap on side and Drax/Gamora on the other and near the end I was usually 1-2 attack shy of being able to hit Thanos twice a turn, on both sides. I may have been able to end it sooner, but I had to keep focusing on the city to make sure I didn't lose to the scheme. I tried my best to prevent escapes, but the twists wouldn't allow for 0. I was able to prevent 0 stones from escaping so Thanos never really got over buffed.

Really fun set up. Thoroughly enjoyed both games. WandaVision & High Tech Spidey got a very favorable Villain deck shuffle, with lots of Henchmen & Salvagers in the early game. Early sacrifices from Vision helped build powerful, trimmed decks. The only low point really, was when I chose to hit Thanos with 2 Villains in the city. The next turn brought a cascade of 4 Omnicraft causing Gef to escape on the Ravager ship (the elusive Gef was 1st to escape in both games). Thanos promptly took the ship from him, and that +3 Attack stalled me a bit. Then a Strike grabbed the Stone-keeper Red Skull out of the city too (the only other escape for that game). Bruce and T’Challa’s game was a lot less friendly, with a run of Twists and the Time Gem before I’d even cycled both decks! I did end up with slightly more VP (mainly thanks to A Day Unlike Any Other and As Usual I Did All the Work), but 3 turns longer and FIVE more escapes!!

The Hero deck led me to a bunch of Iron Man cards on both sides, which was good since many Tech heroes were available to me from my selection. Player 1 was able to nab both Iron Man and Gamora's ults, while the other side picked up 2 of Iron Man's uncommon. Both sides did pretty well, each hitting Thanos at least a few times each, and while I did end up with a handful of escapes, only 1 Infinity Stone got away.

column27: Gamora threw me off my game since I was using all avengers heroes 🥺

First game was just bad luck, hit a couple pockets of scheme twists and was dead on turn 15. I switched Captain Marvel Out for Rocket and Groot. Captain Marvel deals with the end game a lot and this scheme does add another villain group and the schemes twists just keep going back in the villain deck. Game two went great. Captain America and Black Panther slap, if I got below 4 on captain America's Common Cards I had to do a double take cause it rarely happened. Solid gameplay

I went in thinking "Oh 11 fight thats not too bad" and "A scheme where I can some what fight ok." I saw Captain America and Gamora then thought "Ok I'll just pump CA with BP and Artifacts with Star Lord." Well that would have worked if in game 1 CA had shown up at all before turn 20 (I legit thought I had forgotten him like Thanos last week) and if I had had any artifacts show up at all in game 2. Ultimately I tried my best and won. It was really fun hopefully I'm in the top half but I won't be surprised if not.

First i conceded at turn 7, i had 3 twists, an escape and dead hq by that point. The second went a lot better, Jessica's rare tucked 2 twists, so i managed to win before unveiling. Cuckoos did all the work, however "pricey" side managed to get all the 12 mm vp ^^

column27: Unveiled Scheme? More like Unnecessary Scheme! Rictor and Shatterstar teamed up to to shatter their way past Malekith’s villainous weapons and take him out before the 4th Twist was even drawn. Left side stuck to those chosen Heroes, while the right built a solid deck with the 3 pre-selected ones. Had every intention of ditching Squirrel Girls, but either the HQ was stale or she kept being useful! Shatter Team took out a double-weaponed Malekith to draw the only non-weapon Tactic, and the other side just so happened to have 8 Attack the next turn. It was a blast!

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: I love Beta Ray Bill and I love Multiple Man, so it's great to get to use both of them in the same setup and have them both pop off like this. I won the exact turn the Scheme got Unveiled, and I was close to asking in the Discord what I could do if I won before the Scheme got Unveiled.

Played through this one twice and first game won in a romp without the unveiled scheme being revealed. Second game was way more of slog, but when I compared scores I was pretty surprised that #2 was probably the stronger game. Fun setup and combining the least fantastic world (Noir) with the most (Heroes of Asgard) was a fun juxtaposition.

column27: Was really surprised I was able to get both Beta Ray and Shatter Star's rare as early as I did and also was able to get Luke Cage Noir's rare within the first 15-20 turns of the game which with all the KO's I was able to apply to thin the deck both decks were optimized to where Malekith is crying himself to sleep in the brig of the Skuttlebutt.

column27: Nice game, some of the heroes I had never played with before (switcheroo was new for me). Got a bit lucky with how the scheme twists fell and was able to investigate some to the bottom of the villain deck with Jessica Jones' rare. No twists stacked next to the mastermind when the scheme unveiled made the end a breeze!

Red, Green, Yellow heroes (Mainly) with costs of 4 plus made a great deck while the other side focused on discard. Plus I got to use Squirrel girls rare effect! I've never done that and getting to put a SHIELD officer into the hero deck was fun.

column27: I was so close to forgetting that M was an option but I remembered to check the Master Strike link. Saved my game! I picked Man Thing for the teleport ability and it paid off. The Warriors Three would have been my other option.

column27: Fun game! Some very good luck with the VD allowed me to really hone my decks, and they both ended up taking off. Left side was a Luke Cage deck that just did its thing, culminating in a huge “Weight of the World” that finished Malekith. The right side was a Worthy Thrown Artifact deck that put up a couple huge turns, including hitting Mal 2x in one turn. Jessica Jones was clutch, allowing me to Switcheroo into the 4 and 5 costs that I needed for each side. Also, 3 rares in HQ for the first 2 unveiled twists really ensured I was in a good place to not lose to the scheme.

column2: Loki Bathes Earth in Cosmic Rays
column27: Really needed the wound mitigation here.  Just so many different sources...including self inflicted ones via Hulk

Mr. Fantastic was basically a mistake, but I wasn't going to NOT throw in Black Widow in a game featuring Deadpool and the Invisible Woman. I almost did a Hulk/Human Torch setup to answer Loki's Master Strikes but I knew that if I didn't use BW there'd be no chance for me in terms of VP and VP/turn.
Tough decisions between the two attempts- try 2 was 4 turns faster, but try 1 was MUCH higher points thanks to Emma's often maligned Shadowed Thoughts. We'll see if I picked right!
I used black widow and hulk from core the sun is going down big guy.

Really bad luck on attempt 1, but attempt 2 was great.  The Howard and super skrull deck got rolling and never looked back.  The FF deck with warriors 3 didn't ever take off.  Lots of fun, and redeeming after the luck with Mole man.

In our first run Galactus had consumed most of the city before the final scheme. We were slowed down by not having enough KO power in that deck. Round two FF united was running on all cylinders with a streamlined deck of 10 cards and able to draw it all each turn. We could hit the MM twice a turn in the end.
FFU and The Warriors Three worked outstanding together.
We could have done a better job pick heroes that work good together for both games
We saw 4 out of five rares in the hq at the same time. Didn’t stop us though.
bad first game, did well on game two

column2: Mr. Sinister's Secret Invasion of the Skrull Shapeshifters
column27: This setup felt like it limited VP available. With the heroes in the villain deck, along with twists, forced the use of attacks to generate 0 VP. Jean Grey was tailor made to defeat Mr. Sinister, and she sure did that here. My first attempt went very heavy on card draw, and came very close to my second attempt, but I had an unlucky escape early.
Fun!
Always have fun playing with Iceman. Got ran over at the start of game 1 with the city quickly filling with 6-7 power villains.

column22: I did not play a second attempt.
column23: I did not play a second attempt.
column27: Iceman is just sooooo powerful, and even easier to build when his rare and uncommons dropped in the city as Skrulls

MCU side was Iceman/Jean Grey ranged/draw silliness, Comic side was Iron Man/Black Widow draw/rescue/Covert Ops silliness.  Second attempt was slightly faster and had more villains than heroes come out of the villain deck, unfortunately 
both had one escape.
Fun setup. Once you get going with the ranged attack from iceman and cyclops then keep rescuing bystanders with Jean grey and have griot give you extra cards it’s a lock.

Huge deck; draw everything game plan! A bit bummed my first play through had such a big gap between Player 1/2, but still chose it for the quick win. The second one was a ton of fun getting powerful cards, saving bystanders left and right and at the end out drawing my deck. 28 bystanders saved on play through two. I can just picture Black Widow patting baby Groot on the head at the end saying. "Good job little guy."
This was another really fun scenario.  One of my favorite parts of the tournament is having to think through who to include on the team.
